nameMeanings.name: Viall
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Vial
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Emile, Jacques, Veronique.

English (Cornwall) and French: from a personal name derived from Latin Vitalis (see Vitale ). The name became common in England after the Norman Conquest both in its learned form Vitalis and in the northern French form Viel.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Viall was born in 1530, in Luddington in the Brook, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Thomas Viall, was 30 and his mother, Eleasabeth Viall, was 30. He had at least 2 sons with Elizabeth. He died in 1580, in his hometown, at the age of 50.
